nits: s3Key Zod refine only blocks CR/LF, not `"` or `\`. When attachment.filename is omitted, derived filename = s3Key.split('/').pop() bypasses FilenameSchema. No CRLF injection (still blocked), but produces malformed MIME params. → Fixed in follow-up edit: derived filename now stripped via `.replace(/[\r\n"\\]/g, '_')` before interpolation.
att.contentType flows from S3 metadata into header value without explicit validation; bucket is workspace-owned so trust holds, but a sanitization pass would be defense-in-depth.
verify.log out of date (cycle 2 added 2 new tests). → Fixed.
fixes_applied_between_cycles: FilenameSchema (and AttachmentSchema.s3Key) now reject CR/LF; FilenameSchema additionally rejects `"` and `\`.
MIME body part switched from `Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit` to `base64` with 76-char folding so UTF-8 / non-ASCII bodies don't violate 7bit rules.
Derived filename (when att.filename absent) sanitized via `.replace(/[\r\n"\\]/g, '_')`.
Two new vitest cases under 'header injection guards' assert SES.send is not called when subject or filename carries CRLF / quotes / backslash.
